mission.title: open-webui: issue: When system-prompt in OpenAI model is set, there will be an error 400
mission.description: issue: When system-prompt in OpenAI model is set, there will be an error 400. This issue affects 7+ users.

steps.title: Identify open-webui troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your open-webui deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui -n open-webui --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review open-webui configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant open-webui configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui
kubectl get configmap -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=open-webui
```
### Check Existing Issues

### Installation Method

Git Clone

### Open WebUI Version

0.6.12

### Ollama Version (if applicable)

_No response_

### Operating System

Docker

### Browser (if applicable)

_No response_

### Confirmation

- Start

steps.title: Apply the fix for issue: When system-prompt in OpenAI model is set, there…
steps.description:
> Not anymore. Do not enable Ollama params for OpenAI models, this behaviour change is required to better support OpenAI compatible LLM providers (e.g. vLLM)

Then what do I do if I use both local and OpenAI models? Do I have to set them for every one of my ~30 local models now? If that's the case would you consider adding an explicit "ignore" option for the model advanced parameters so that parameters set to it will not get added? Doing this for all my current and future local models is

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Confirm issue: When system-prompt in OpenAI model is set,… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui -n open-webui --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n open-webui --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with open-webui installed or the issue environment reproducible.
